description: Prompt Architecture Unification — Fix the split prompt pipeline so language learning directives (CEFR modes, vocabulary frequency, scaffolding, critical language rule) actually reach the LLM on all conversation paths (SSE, WS, NPC-NPC), fix language learning detection fallback, add Vite WS proxy, and send game state from client to server

userStories.title: Accept and use client-provided systemPrompt on SSE path
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the /api/conversation/stream endpoint to use the client-provided system prompt when present so that language learning directives actually reach the LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: In http-bridge.ts, read systemPrompt from req.body and pass it through to streamTextResponse()
In streamTextResponse(), if a systemPrompt string is provided AND the session needs a context rebuild, use it as the system prompt instead of calling buildContext()
If systemPrompt is NOT provided (e.g., non-game clients), fall back to buildContext() as before
Preserve the existing characterName, worldContext, and characterGender fields in conversationContext by extracting them from the buildContext() result or the session
Add a log: '[ConversationBridge] Using client-provided system prompt (N chars)' vs '[ConversationBridge] Using server-built system prompt'
Add test: POST to /api/conversation/stream with a systemPrompt field, verify the LLM receives that prompt (not the server-built one)
Add test: POST without systemPrompt, verify fallback to buildContext()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The client already sends systemPrompt in the SSE request body (server-chat-provider.ts:298). The server just doesn't read it. streamTextResponse() signature needs a new optional clientSystemPrompt parameter. The session caching logic (needsRebuild at line 109) should still work — if the client prompt is present, skip buildContext() entirely for this session turn.

userStories.title: Accept and use client-provided systemPrompt on WS path
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WebSocket bridge to use a client-provided system prompt when present so that language learning directives reach the LLM via the WS transport.
acceptanceCriteria: In ws-bridge.ts handleTextInput(), read systemPrompt from the msg object (the client sends it as textInput.systemPrompt in the WS JSON message)
If systemPrompt is provided and the session needs context rebuild, use it instead of calling buildContext()
If not provided, fall back to buildContext() as before
Add a log: '[WS-Bridge] Using client-provided system prompt (N chars)' vs '[WS-Bridge] Using server-built system prompt'
Update server-chat-provider.ts sendTextWS() to include systemPrompt in the textInput JSON message alongside text, sessionId, characterId, worldId, languageCode
Add test: send a WS textInput message with systemPrompt, verify the LLM receives it
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The WS message format at server-chat-provider.ts:252-257 sends { textInput: { text, sessionId, characterId, worldId, languageCode } }. Add systemPrompt to this object. handleTextInput() at ws-bridge.ts:79 destructures msg — add systemPrompt to the destructure.

userStories.title: Enrich server-side prompt with CEFR language mode directives
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the server-side prompt builder to include CEFR-aware language mode directives as a fallback, so that even when the client prompt is unavailable the NPC still adapts to the player's level.
acceptanceCriteria: In context-manager.ts buildSystemPrompt(), when languageLearning is not null, call assignNPCLanguageMode(characterId, cefrLevel) from shared/language/cefr-adaptation.ts and include the result
Append buildLanguageModeDirective(mode) output to the LANGUAGE LEARNING MODE section (e.g., 'Speak in simplified mode: 5-7 word sentences, high-frequency vocabulary only')
Append buildFrequencyDirective(cefrLevel) output from shared/language/vocabulary-frequency.ts to constrain vocabulary rank

Add the CRITICAL LANGUAGE RULE enforcement at the very end of the prompt: 'CRITICAL LANGUAGE RULE: Your ENTIRE response must be in [targetLanguage]. Do NOT use English. Every word must be in [targetLanguage]. This is non-negotiable.'
Determine CEFR level from player progress data: map profLabel (beginner/intermediate/advanced) to CEFR (A1/B1/B2) or pass through if available from gameState
Add test: build server prompt with languageLearning active and CEFR A1, verify prompt contains 'simplified' directive and frequency constraint '1-200'
Add test: build server prompt with languageLearning active and CEFR B2, verify prompt contains 'natural' directive
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: assignNPCLanguageMode() is in cefr-adaptation.ts:43-54. buildLanguageModeDirective() is in cefr-adaptation.ts:60-96. buildFrequencyDirective() is in vocabulary-frequency.ts. Need to map profLabel to CEFR: beginner -> A1, intermediate -> B1, advanced -> B2. Or accept explicit CEFR from gameState.

userStories.title: Fix language learning detection fallback
userStories.description: As a developer, I need language learning detection to work reliably so that the LANGUAGE LEARNING MODE section is never skipped for language learning worlds.
acceptanceCriteria: In context-manager.ts buildContext() (around line 384), add a fallback: if languages.find(l => l.isLearningTarget) returns null, check world.targetLanguage — if set and not 'English', construct the languageLearning object using world.targetLanguage
Also check world.gameType === 'language-learning' as an additional signal
Log a warning when the fallback is used: '[Context] No isLearningTarget in languages, falling back to world.targetLanguage'
Add test: create context with world.targetLanguage = 'French' but no language with isLearningTarget: true, verify languageLearning is still populated
Add test: create context with world.targetLanguage = null, verify languageLearning is null
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The current code at line 384 does languages.find(l => l.isLearningTarget) which fails if the world has targetLanguage: 'French' but the languages collection doesn't have isLearningTarget: true on the French entry. This is the most likely root cause of the ~481-token prompt with no language directives the user observed.

userStories.title: Send gameState context from client to server on SSE and WS paths
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the client to send critical game state (CEFR level, player vocabulary, grammar patterns) to the server so that the server-side prompt builder can use them even when building its own prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: In server-chat-provider.ts sendTextSSE(), add optional fields to the request body: cefrLevel, playerVocabulary (array of word objects with mastery), playerGrammarPatterns (array of pattern objects with accuracy)
In server-chat-provider.ts sendTextWS(), add the same fields to the textInput JSON message
In BabylonChatPanel.ts, when calling insimulClient.sendText(), pass the CEFR level and game state from languageTracker — extend SendTextOptions in the InsimulClient if needed
In http-bridge.ts, read cefrLevel, playerVocabulary, playerGrammarPatterns from req.body and pass them to buildContext() via the gameState parameter
In ws-bridge.ts, read the same fields from the textInput message and pass them to buildContext()
Add test: POST to /api/conversation/stream with cefrLevel: 'A1' and playerVocabulary, verify the server prompt includes vocabulary review words
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently buildContext() accepts a gameState parameter but only prologFacts is passed. It also supports playerVocabulary, playerGrammarPatterns, playerProgress, activeQuests. This is belt-and-suspenders: even if US-001 makes the server use the client prompt, this ensures the server has the data for a rich fallback.

userStories.title: Add Vite WebSocket proxy for /ws/conversation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Vite dev server to proxy WebSocket connections to the Express backend so that the WS transport works in development without falling back to SSE.
acceptanceCriteria: In vite.config.ts, add a proxy rule for /ws/conversation that forwards WebSocket connections to the Express backend (http://localhost:8000 or the configured port)
The proxy must support WebSocket upgrade (ws: true in Vite proxy config)
After the change, verify that ws://localhost:8080/ws/conversation successfully connects (the dev console no longer shows 'WebSocket connection failed')
Verify the InsimulClient logs '[ChatPanel] InsimulClient ready (chat=server, tts=server)' and the SSE fallback warning does NOT appear
Add comment in vite.config.ts explaining why the WS proxy is needed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently the client connects to ws://localhost:8080/ws/conversation (Vite dev port) but the WS bridge is on the Express server at port 8000. Vite proxy config: '/ws': { target: 'http://localhost:8000', ws: true }. The Express server registers the WS bridge at server/index.ts:604-605.

userStories.title: Consolidate NPC-NPC prompt builder with language learning support
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPC-NPC conversations to include language learning directives so that overheard/eavesdropped NPC conversations are also in the target language.
acceptanceCriteria: In npc-conversation-engine.ts buildNpcNpcSystemPrompt(), add a languageDirective section: if the world has a target language, append 'LANGUAGE: This conversation must be entirely in [targetLanguage]. Both NPCs are native speakers. Write all dialogue in [targetLanguage] only — no English.' to the prompt
Pass the world's target language through to buildNpcNpcSystemPrompt() — identify it from the world's languages collection or targetLanguage field
Verify the generated NPC-NPC exchanges are in the target language when the world has one
Add test: build NPC-NPC prompt with target language 'French', verify prompt contains French language directive
Add test: build NPC-NPC prompt with no target language, verify no language directive added
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently NPC-NPC conversations say 'They speak French and English. Use this language naturally in dialogue.' — this is too vague. Needs a stronger directive matching the player-NPC treatment. The initiateConversation() function at line 567 already extracts worldLangs. Need to also identify which is the learning target.

userStories.title: Add integration test verifying full prompt pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an integration test that verifies the complete prompt pipeline — from client prompt through to the system prompt that reaches the LLM — to prevent future regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a test that simulates a full player-NPC chat exchange via /api/conversation/stream with a systemPrompt provided, and verifies the LLM receives the client-provided system prompt containing 'LANGUAGE LEARNING MODE' or 'CRITICAL LANGUAGE RULE'

Create a test that simulates a chat exchange WITHOUT a client systemPrompt, and verifies the server builds its own prompt with LANGUAGE LEARNING MODE section (when world has target language) including CEFR language mode and frequency directives
Create a test for NPC-NPC conversation verifying the prompt contains target language directive when applicable
All tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These integration tests should mock the LLM provider to capture the system prompt and verify its contents, rather than making real Gemini API calls.
